# Implementation Plan: FridaForge CLI 命令行工具

**Branch**: `001-fridaforge-cli` | **Date**: 2026-05-09 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/001-fridaforge-cli/spec.md`

**Note**: This file is generated by `/speckit.plan`. See `.specify/templates/plan-template.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Build the FridaForge CLI skeleton using Go 1.25 with cobra for command routing, viper for configuration binding, and yaml.v3 for YAML deserialization. The CLI exposes two subcommands: `fridaforge device list` (list connected devices) and `fridaforge spec validate <file>` (validate hook spec YAML). The M1 scope is strictly local: read files, print output, return exit codes — no network, database, or actual Frida connections. The device manager is interface-based with a stub implementation in M1. Parsed spec data is exposed as Go structs via `pkg/spec/` for downstream consumption.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: Go 1.25
**Primary Dependencies**: cobra (github.com/spf13/cobra), viper (github.com/spf13/viper), yaml.v3 (gopkg.in/yaml.v3)
**Storage**: N/A (file-based I/O only)
**Testing**: Go standard library `testing` + table-driven tests (`*_test.go` co-located with source)
**Target Platform**: Linux, macOS, Windows (single binary)
**Project Type**: CLI tool
**Performance Goals**: spec validation <2s, device listing <5s (M1: stub/mock, actual timing deferred to M2)
**Constraints**: Pure local CLI — no network calls, no database, no actual Frida process interaction in M1
**Scale/Scope**: Single user, local workstation; YAML files with O(100) hook targets

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Gate | Chapter | Status | Notes |
|------|---------|--------|-------|
| gofmt formatting | 2.1 | ✅ PLAN | CI will include `gofmt -d`; enforced via Makefile target |
| go vet zero-warning | 2.1 | ✅ PLAN | Included in CI/Makefile |
| golangci-lint | 2.1 | ✅ PLAN | `.golangci.yml` to be created; `golangci-lint run` in CI |
| Package naming: lowercase, singular, no underscores | 2.2 | ✅ PLAN | `cmd/fridaforge`, `pkg/config`, `pkg/spec`, `pkg/device` |
| Export naming: MixedCaps | 2.2 | ✅ PLAN | `NewHookSpec`, `HookTarget`, `ValidateConfig` |
| Error wrapping with `%w` | 2.3 | ✅ PLAN | All errors wrapped with context via `fmt.Errorf("...: %w", err)` |
| No bare panic | 2.3 | ✅ PLAN | Only `log.Fatal` in `main.go`; library code returns errors |
| Concurrency: context.Context lifecycle | 2.4 | ⏸️ N/A (M1) | M1 has no goroutines; deferred to M2 |
| Testing: table-driven, `*_test.go` co-located, ≥80% coverage | 2.5 | ✅ PLAN | Table-driven tests for all exported functions |
| Go doc comments on all exports | 2.6 | ✅ PLAN | All exported types/functions must have Go doc comments |
| Ethical disclaimer on first run | 3.4 | ✅ PLAN | `PersistentPreRun` hook on root command: check for marker file, prompt if absent |
| Frida ops via FridaDeviceManager | 3.1 | ⏸️ DEFERRED (M2) | M1 uses `DeviceManager` interface with stub; actual Frida integration in M2 |
| SpecKit workflow: no skip clarify/analyze | 5.2 | ✅ PLAN | M1 follows full SpecKit cycle (specify → clarify → plan → tasks → analyze → implement) |
| Commit per task | 5.2 | ✅ PLAN | One Git commit per completed task from tasks.md |

**Gate Result**: ✅ PASS — All applicable gates satisfied. Two gates deferred to M2 (concurrency, Frida process isolation) as M1 scope has no goroutines or actual Frida connections.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-fridaforge-cli/
├── plan.md              # This file
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output — CLI & YAML contracts
│   ├── cli-commands.md
│   └── yaml-schema.md
├── checklists/
│   └── requirements.md  # From /speckit.specify
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks — NOT created here)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
cmd/
└── fridaforge/
    ├── main.go              # Root cobra command, entry point, ethical disclaimer
    ├── device.go            # `device list` subcommand
    └── spec.go              # `spec validate` subcommand
pkg/
├── config/
│   ├── loader.go            # YAML file loading + viper integration
│   ├── loader_test.go
│   ├── validator.go         # HookSpec structural validation
│   └── validator_test.go
├── spec/
│   ├── types.go             # HookSpec, HookTarget, HookType structs + yaml tags
│   ├── types_test.go
│   ├── errors.go            # Validation error types
│   └── errors_test.go
└── device/
    ├── types.go             # Device struct
    ├── manager.go           # DeviceManager interface + stub implementation
    └── manager_test.go
```

**Structure Decision**: Go standard project layout. `cmd/` contains the single CLI binary entry point. `pkg/` holds reusable library packages organized by domain: `config` (file I/O + validation), `spec` (data models), `device` (device discovery abstraction). This maps directly to the M1 deliverables specified in milestones.md: `cmd/fridaforge/`, `pkg/config/`, `pkg/spec/`.
